actionable_feedback: Fix the fail/warn/status logic in brine_scaling_model.xlsx so the scenario status is computed directly from flags: fail = (pressure_adj > limit) OR (scale_index > 2.3); warn = (pressure_adj <= limit) AND (limit - pressure_adj <= 5). Remove any manual labeling that can become inconsistent with computed flags.
Re-run all scenario rows and update package_results so status entries match the computed per-well flags. Ensure any rounding/formatting does not hide scale_index > 2.3 conditions.

Export the core calculation ranges (pressure_adj, scale_index, per-well warn/fail flags, scenario status, and seven_day_loss_capped) to a CSV or take high-resolution screenshots of the workbook ranges showing formulas and values for each scenario. Attach these to the revised submission.

Ensure the reinjection_decision_memo.md is submitted fully (untruncated). If automated previews truncate the memo, also attach a plain .txt copy or include the relevant scenario table inline in the CSV to enable automated reviewers to parse it.

Confirm 7-day loss capping is implemented as described (loss_capped_7d = MIN(loss_raw_7d, 42,000*7)), and show a sample calculation (cell formulas or a short table) demonstrating the cap applied for at least one capped scenario (e.g., I1+I2+I3).
artifact_specific_comments: brine_scaling_model.xlsx: The model formulas are documented in the memo, but the final scenario status column appears inconsistent with computed scale_index/pressure_adj values. Because openpyxl was unavailable for parsing, graders could not confirm cell-level formulas; provide CSV or screenshot exports of the calculation ranges with visible formulas.

reinjection_decision_memo.md: Memo contains the operational formulas and a scenario table, but the preview is truncated in this environment. Provide an untruncated memo or duplicate the scenario table into the CSV/screenshot to ensure all rows are verifiable.
feedback_summary: Artifacts are present and the memo documents the model formulas and scenario table. However, semantic grading and the verifier found an internal inconsistency: several scenarios (e.g., I2, I3) are labeled PASS in package_results/memo despite the memo-quoted per-well scale_index exceeding the fail threshold (scale_index > 2.3). The spreadsheet could not be parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), so graders relied on the memo preview and metadata. Fix the fail/warn labeling so it is derived programmatically from computed flags, and provide cell-level evidence (CSV export or screenshots) plus an untruncated memo for re-verification.

limitations_noted_by_grader: Spreadsheet (.xlsx) could not be parsed in grader environment (openpyxl unavailable); semantic judgement relied on memo preview and metadata.
Memo preview truncated in places, limiting full automated verification of some scenario rows.
recommended_evidence_to_attach: CSV export of core calculation ranges (one row per scenario showing pressure_adj, scale_index for each well, per-well warn/fail flags, scenario status, loss_raw_7d, loss_capped_7d).
High-resolution screenshots showing the exact cell formulas and results for the ranges used to compute pressure_adj, scale_index, fail/warn flags, and capped losses.
Untruncated reinjection_decision_memo.md (or plain .txt copy) if preview truncation persists.
recommended_next_steps_for_reviewer: Open provided CSV/screenshots to verify fail/warn formulas and relabeled scenario statuses.
Confirm the 7-day loss capping is implemented and applied to capped scenarios.
extra_model_fields.verifier_findings_summary: Verifier confirmed artifact presence and the grader's semantic downgrade due to an internal inconsistency (numeric intermediates vs status labels). Re-run required after providing cell-level evidence.

trace_specific_comments: Trace shows manual OpenXML (.xlsx) creation and an earlier off-by-one row-index error that was corrected. This history suggests formula references may have been hand-assembled and are therefore susceptible to indexing/labeling mistakes; prioritise programmatic verification of row mappings and include evidence of corrected mappings in the revision.
Trace steps 3-7 document dependency and generation workarounds; include the exported CSV/screenshot to remove reliance on environment-specific parsers during verification.
